How It Works

Detailed explanation of the mechanism, algorithm, or protocol. This is where the focus page adds value over the brief L2 mention. Anchor every paragraph to concrete symbols, tables, masks, counters, or states from the real code.

Phase/Step 2

Behavior Diagram

stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Active : trigger
    Active --> Processing : start
    Processing --> Done : complete
    Processing --> Error : fail
    Error --> Idle : reset
    Done --> Idle : next

Replace with description of state transitions or message sequences specific to this topic.

Writing target: this section should explain dynamic behavior that a reader would otherwise have to reconstruct by reading several functions side by side.

Annotated Code Walkthrough

Walk through the actual source code step by step, with explanations between each snippet.

First, the entry point receives the input and validates it:

// Replace with real code snippet from source
void entry_point(Input& input) {
    if (!validate(input)) return;
    // ...
}

Then the core processing happens here — note the key decision point:

// Next real code snippet
Result process(Input& input) {
    // The critical branch:
    if (input.type == FAST_PATH) {
        return fast_process(input);
    }
    return slow_process(input);
}
Performance note: The fast path avoids X overhead. The slow path is taken in ~Y% of cases.
